things.name: Fuggerei
things.description: The world's oldest social housing estate, built by banker Jakob Fugger in 1516; 67 houses with 140 apartments still rented for a symbolic 88 cents per year

things.name: Augsburg Cathedral
things.description: Magnificent Romanesque-Gothic cathedral with the world's oldest figurative stained glass windows from 1065 and a famous bronze door from the 11th century

things.name: Augsburg Town Hall and Perlachturm
things.description: Elias Holl's magnificent Renaissance Town Hall from 1624 with its extraordinary Golden Hall; climb the adjacent medieval Perlachturm for panoramic views

things.name: Augsburg Water Management System (UNESCO)
things.description: UNESCO World Heritage water management system of canals, towers and water towers built from the 13th century; some of the finest Renaissance fountains in Europe

things.name: Maximilian Museum
things.description: Outstanding city history and applied arts museum in a Renaissance patrician palace with exceptional collections of goldsmithery and civic history

things.name: Mozart Birthplace Museum
things.description: Museum in the house where Leopold Mozart, father of Wolfgang Amadeus, was born in 1719; a fascinating window into the musical dynasty's Augsburg roots

things.name: Brechthaus
things.description: Birthplace of playwright Bertolt Brecht in 1898, now a museum; Brecht is one of the 20th century's greatest dramatists and Augsburg's most famous native

things.name: Schaezlerpalais (Baroque Palace)
things.description: Splendid 18th-century baroque palace with a ballroom where Mozart performed; now housing the German Baroque Gallery of Old Masters paintings

things.name: Augsburg Zoo
things.description: Established zoo in parkland south of the city centre with over 1,700 animals from 280 species including jaguars, orangutans and Nile hippos

things.name: WWK Arena
things.description: Home of FC Augsburg with a 30,660 capacity; the club competed in the UEFA Europa League in 2015-16, Augsburg's first-ever European campaign
